meta_title: For Students: design with AI to Solve no project ideas (Advanced Comparison)
meta_description: Learn how to design with AI and overcome no project ideas. A Advanced-level Comparison using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: Design with AI to Solve No Project Ideas
content.seo_description: Overcome the 'no project ideas' trap. Learn to design with AI using Antigravity and Claude Code to build professional-grade SaaS projects for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: Stop waiting for inspiration and start engineering. By leveraging Antigravity for rapid architectural scaffolding and Claude Code for iterative implementation, you can transform abstract concepts into functional SaaS products. This workflow bridges the gap between academic theory and industry-standard deployment, ensuring every project you build is resume-ready and scalable.
content.intro:
The blank editor is the graveyard of ambition. You spend hours scrolling GitHub, waiting for a 'perfect' project idea that never arrives, while your technical skills stagnate in a cycle of tutorials. You aren't lacking talent; you are lacking a systematic framework to turn raw ambition into shipping code.

Design with AI is not about letting an LLM do the work for you; it is about offloading the cognitive load of boilerplate architecture so you can focus on complex logic. By treating your code as an evolving organism rather than a static file, you move from a student struggling to 'start' to an architect capable of building production-ready SaaS applications.

steps.title: Architecting the Product Vision
steps.description: Define the problem space. Use AI to identify market gaps that offer enough complexity to look good on a resume but enough focus to finish in a week.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Act as a Senior SaaS Architect. Generate a list of 5 high-impact, niche B2B SaaS project ideas that solve specific problems for small business owners. For each, outline the core data schema and the primary user flow.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --init 'Create a project roadmap document outlining the MVP features for [Chosen Idea].'

steps.title: Bootstrap the Tech Stack
steps.description: Don't waste time on folder structures. Use AI to scaffold a modern stack (Next.js, Tailwind, Supabase) that follows industry best practices.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a scaffold for a Next.js 14 application with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, and Supabase integration. Include a clean directory structure for components, hooks, and services.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Generate directory structure and initialize project configuration files for a standard SaaS boilerplate.'

steps.title: Iterative Feature Implementation
steps.description: Build the core functionality. Focus on one high-value feature at a time to maintain momentum and avoid scope creep.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a clean, modular React component for a user authentication dashboard. Ensure it uses Zod for schema validation and handles error states gracefully.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --add 'Implement the user registration flow with Supabase Auth and Zod validation in the auth/register route.'

steps.title: Testing and Refinement
steps.description: Use Claude Code to automate unit testing. A project without tests is just a prototype; a project with tests is a product.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vitest suite for the authentication service. Include tests for successful login, invalid credentials, and database connection timeouts.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --test 'Analyze the auth service and generate a comprehensive Vitest suite for all edge cases.'

steps.title: Deployment and Documentation
steps.description: Prepare your code for the real world. Automate the deployment pipeline so you can show off a live URL on your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a Vercel deployment configuration file and write a professional README.md that explains the project's architecture, stack, and setup instructions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude --run 'Create a professional README.md and a vercel.json configuration file for production deployment.'

pro_tips: Treat your AI as a junior developer: always verify the logic, never copy-paste blindly.
Use 'Chain-of-Thought' prompting to force the AI to explain its architectural decisions before writing code.
Modularize everything; if a component exceeds 100 lines, ask the AI to refactor it into smaller, testable modules.
Build 'Error Handling' into the prompt: explicitly ask the AI to write comprehensive try/catch blocks for every database interaction.
Document your AI prompts in a 'prompt-library' folder within your repo to track how your build process evolves over time.
content.next_steps: After completing your MVP, push the repository to GitHub, deploy to Vercel, and write a 'Lessons Learned' blog post to link in your portfolio.
content.conversion_block: You now have the framework to stop dreaming and start shipping. The industry doesn't need more tutorial-followers; it needs builders who know how to leverage modern tools to solve real problems. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
